critic_raw: The design uses 'agent experience level (tenure band)' as a metadata feature appended as text. Using tenure band as text is unusual — typically this would be a numerical or categorical feature, not text. If the transformer processes this as text (e.g., 'tenure: 3-5 years'), it must learn the semantic meaning of tenure from fine-tuning on training data. The TF-IDF baseline would also receive this as text features. Are both models processing tenure band equivalently?
defender_raw: Round 1: Appending metadata as text to the thread is a common technique in transformer-based classification — it allows the model to process both content and context in a single encoder pass. 'Tenure: 3-5 years' as text would receive a consistent token sequence that the transformer can learn to interpret during fine-tuning. The TF-IDF baseline also receives the same concatenated text (including tenure band text), so both models encode tenure band equivalently — via their respective text representations. If the TF-IDF model has high IDF weight on tenure-specific vocabulary, it would use tenure as a feature. The comparison is fair because both models process the same text input.

rounds.critic_followup: If tenure band vocabulary appears rarely in the text (e.g., 'tenure: 3-5 years' is only in the metadata header), TF-IDF would give it high weight (rare across documents). This could artificially inflate TF-IDF performance for the tenure feature. Does the 'min_df=2' TF-IDF parameter prevent this?
rounds.defender_followup: The TF-IDF parameters (min_df=2, max_df=0.95) filter out terms appearing in fewer than 2 documents or more than 95% of documents. Tenure band text would appear in all tickets (since metadata is appended to all), so it would have high df and potentially be filtered by max_df if the exact token combination is too common. The TF-IDF implementation for text metadata appended to all tickets would actually have high document frequency, giving lower IDF weight — not inflating performance. The min_df and max_df parameters ensure the vocabulary focuses on terms with informative frequency distributions. This is handled correctly.

adjudication_raw: One concern: tenure band as text may be processed differently by TF-IDF vs. transformer. Resolved: both models receive same text; TF-IDF frequency parameters handle common metadata tokens appropriately. Verdict: defense_wins.
